headline: Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings (NCLH) Is Up 5.0% After EPS Cut, Activist Deal And Cost Pivot - Has The Bull Case Changed?
summary: In early May 2026, Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings reported first-quarter results that exceeded earnings expectations, outlined US$125 million in SG&A run-rate savings, and announced a cooperation agreement with activist investor Elliott Management that reshaped its board oversight. Soon after, law firm Pomerantz launched a securities investigation following Norwegian’s reduced full-year adjusted EPS guidance, even as multiple company directors bought shares in the open market, highlighting a...
